text: To set effective project goals, use the SMART framework. This means goals should be Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of "improve the website," set a goal like "increase page load speed by 20% within three months." Break your project into milestones, which are key checkpoints that show progress. Each milestone should have clear deliverables, such as a completed report or a prototype. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment by discussing goals with everyone involved. This keeps the team focused and avoids misunderstandings. When goals are clear, the whole project runs more smoothly.
